titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the John Nathaniel Vincent Papers PASC-M.0031
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by UCLA Library Special Collections staff.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-13 15:24:08 -0700.

did.abstract: This collection consists of sketches, holographs, and transparencies of musical works.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. Portions of collection unprocessed and unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Reproduction and Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

odd.head: UCLA Catalog Record ID
odd.p: UCLA Catalog Record ID: 9942339303606533

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], John Nathaniel Vincent Papers (Collection PASC-M 31). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: This collection was acquired by the UCLA Music Library. When the Music Library special collections was merged under the umbrella of UCLA Library Special Collections, this collection was transferred to LSC.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biography
bioghist.p: Vincent was born on May 17, 1902 in Birmingham, AL; diploma from New England Conservatory of Music; BS and MA from George Peabody College; studied with Walter Piston at Harvard Univ., 1933-35, where he received the John Knowles Paine Travelling Fellowship for two years' study with Nadia Boulanger; Ph.D, Cornell Univ., 1942; head of music dept. at Western Kentucky State Univ., 1937-45, then succeeded Arnold Schoenberg as professor of composition at UCLA, 1946-69; conducted orchestras throughout the US and South America; director, Huntington Hartford Foundation, 1952-65; wrote The diatonic modes in modern music (1951); composed many works, including: Three jacks ballet (1941), Primeval void (opera buffa, 1969-71), Symphony in D (1954), Stabat mater (1970), Three Grecian songs, and two string quartets; he died on Jan. 21, 1977 in Santa Monica, CA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of sketches, holographs and transparencies of musical works by John Nathaniel Vincent. Also includes correspondence, class lecture notes, research materials and notes, programs, UCLA Music Dept. documents, financial and personal records, photographs, commercial and non-commercial disc recordings of music by Vincent, and miscellaneous memorabilia.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Biographical materials (boxes 1-3)
Documents relating to musical works (boxes 4,8-10)
Programs, reviews, notes (boxes 5-7)
Articles, speeches, papers (boxes 11-13)
International travels (boxes 14-16)
Correspondence (boxes 17-31,44-47)
Music festivals and performing organizations (boxes 32-43)
UCLA and other academic institutions (boxes 48-56)
Radio broadcast records (boxes 57-59)
Libretti and musical notes (boxes 60-61)
Musical sketches and holographs (boxes 62-72)
Transparent masters of musical works (boxes 73-77)
Notes for the counterpoint and harmony books (boxes 78-80)
The diatonic modes (boxes 81-86)
Financial and personal records (boxes 90-92)
Program books, scrapbooks (boxes 93-95)
Tape recordings of Vincent's music performances, interviews (boxes 96-97)
Memorabilia (boxes 98,101,105)
Miscellaneous (boxes 99-100)
Disc recordings of Vincent's works (boxes 102,104)
Vincent's lectures (box 103)
Music manuscripts (boxes 106-122)
Posters (packages 1-5)

relatedmaterial.head: Related Material
relatedmaterial.p: Flautist, composer, teacher [oral history transcript] / John Vincent, interviewee. UCLA Oral History Department interview, c1985. Available at UCLA Library Special Collections.

controlaccess.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles. Department of Music--Faculty -- Archives

did.physdesc: Church programs, TV Guide, script for interview

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Unpublished materials, Magazine and Newspaper Articles, Book citations, Encyclopedia Articles, Miscellaneous.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Information about Vincent's upcoming interview on Creative People including the TV Guide for that week and a church bulletin. Also includes typewritten script for the televised interview. Also contains miscellaneous handwritten notes.

did.physdesc: Membership cards, letters, receipts, and other proof of memberships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Membership information for various organizations, including the Opera Guild, ASCAP, American Music Center, etc.

did.unittitle: Composition Teaching: Philosophy of; Remarks by; Students
did.physdesc: Newspaper clippings, programs, and handwritten notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Composition teaching memorabilia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Reproduced correspondence and reviews regarding Vincent's compositions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Articles about Vincent that were not officially published.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Full catalog of Vincent's works along with a brief biographical sketch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from John Vincent to Samuels.

did.physdesc: Original and carbon copies of letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters from George Sander. Some handwritten and some typed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Note about Solti, written by either John or Ruth Vincent.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Solti was LA Philharmonic conductor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Copies of letters from JV and original letters to him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two carbon copies of a letter from JV to DS-E and a postcard from DS-E. Postcard has annotation from Ruth Vincent on it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from Barbara (Ziesl) Schoenberg to JV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from HS as well as note about him by JV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from ES to Ruth Vincent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Christmas poem from Edna and Yu Shan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Holiday card from Smith family.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Mentions performance by Philadelphia Symphony of JV's Symphony in D.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Holiday poem by LS, signed Season's Greetings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters regarding Mark Stack's potential apartment in New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Personal letters from SS to JV.

did.unittitle: Stein, Sophie R. (KPFK director for Young People)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from SS to Ruth Vincent regarding radio chess lessons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters to and from WS, at the time president of Lincoln Center. Programs from UCLA events relevant to WS (his pieces etc).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes awards, commissions, conducting/performing, libraries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from Vincent to Sandi.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between JV and LS regarding positions for LS. Includes programs and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Documents to and from Clarence Sawhill, who was the acting chair of UCLA's music department. Includes a photocopy of his obituary from 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from Schaller regarding new musical technique.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from RS regarding JV's symphony.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from ES regarding upcoming performance of JV work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Handwritten letters from MS to Ruth Vincent, John's wife.

did.physdesc: Original and carbon copies of letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters regarding commissioned piece by JV for the University of Kentucky, School of Fine Arts.

did.physdesc: Original and carbon copies of letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding a bequest to the National Assoc. of American Composers and Conductors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters relating to Wolfgang Schubert, who sought employment at UCLA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from HS regarding JV's Nacre.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from AS regarding a University Symphony Orchestra (UCLA) performance conducted by JV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two letters from HS regarding possible collaboration between JV and the Kansas City Philharmonic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters between HS and JV. HS was a conductor for Columbia's Music Department.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding musical collaborations and performances.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One letter from ES regarding Dutch orchestras and one letter from the UK's Royal College of Music regarding ES's upcoming appointment there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Simon's performance of JV's La Jolla Concerto.

did.unittitle: Singer, Jacques (music director and conductor, Portland Symphony)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from JS regarding works by JV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter to JV regarding the Huntington Foundation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Sister Rose's possible involvement at the Chicago Musical College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding JV's potential work at Colgate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and clippings regarding Slonimsky and Vincent. Includes letters of recommendation by NS for JV's potential grants.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Slonimsky edited Die Musik in Geschichte und Gegenwart and included biographical info on JV in the 1966 edition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two letters regarding JV's Meditation work. Each letter dated "Friday morning," with no month, day, or year.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Letters written on behalf of Eugene Ormandy, Music Director, Philadelphia Orchestra Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter to Chard Smith sent on to JV. Letter from someone at the [London] Times. Signature is illegible.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter of introduction from Moses Smith (music editor, Boston Evening Telegraph) for JV to write music reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from RS (LA County Museum of Art).

did.physdesc: Original and copied letters and programs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters and programs related to Nikolai Sokoloff and the Musical Arts Society of La Jolla

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding Atlanta Symphony Orchestra. Calendar for 1963-4 season.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter in German from Herbert Spitz

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Letter apologizes for a mistake made by HS.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding JV's potential appointment at Western Kentucky Teachers College.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: RS was president of UC Berkeley.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding performances at George Pepperdine College in LA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between JV and WS.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: William Steinberg was Musical Director and Conductor, The Pittsburgh Symphony Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letters regarding visas to travel to Russia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between JV and RS

odd.head: General note
odd.p: RS was professor of music at UCLA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 38 folders of correspondence and memorabilia.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Includes folders of Eugene Ormandy and Richard Nixon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 65 folders of correspondence and memorabilia.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Includes folder of Jan Popper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Obituary of Schonbach, who was an LA Philharmonic violist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Originals and copies of articles and programs related to Schoenberg.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Includes program from 1956 performance in honor of UCLA's music building being named for Schoenberg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from GS, program from Lincoln Center (Festival 1967) and news clipping regarding Schuller's opera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Unsigned holiday card from JS and obituary for him.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: JS was a cellist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Letter from RS to JV; Time article about RS.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: At time, RS was artistic director of the Marlboro Music Festival

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Program from: A Festival of Music at Occidental College. Roger Sessions spoke there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Catalog for watercolor show by Millard Sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Business card of Tibor Shik's with handwritten directions to his residence in Hollywood.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Spanish language program of the Cuarteto Lener, of which Joseph Smilovits was 2nd violin.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Has handwritten notes by JV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of book cover: God and Mr. Gomez by Jack Smith

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Subtitle: The hilarious account of how the author and his wife learn that it takes God and Mr. Gomez to build a vacation dream house in Baja California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Article by Russell Smith: The New Music

did.physdesc: Copy of letter and news clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Copy of letter to Proctor Stafford (art collector) and news clipping about his West Mexican art.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Letter: 1954 Article: 1977

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Article entitled: Stevens Stays in Arts Business

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Stevens was ex-chairman of the National Council of the Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Published music (not by Vincent), in some cases autographed by the composer for Vincent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Printed music (published by Highland Music Company) of Violin Classics edited and arranged by Diana Steiner, autographed by D. Steiner for John Vincent.

scopecontent.p: Original sketches and drafts of Symphony in D

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note

did.unittitle: Variation on a Theme of Yulysses Kay MS Copies and photostat copies [Kay's original theme]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One master with photocopies.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Original says: Happy Birthday, Ulysses! And all good wishes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes: vellum of Quarteto Iimo, sketch entitled "showing process of 'decoding'", photocopies of first Violin part, Violincello, second violin, second violino, personal copy of Suite transcription for string orchestra with obligato for glass armonica, and pencil sketches of piano part

did.unittitle: Music for Redcross film (1948) [manuscript pencil score]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Single copy of "Music for Redcross film" scored for piano.

did.unittitle: Music for Holiday House (for strings)[completed in Los Angeles on June 25, 1948]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Single, ink manuscript of Music for Holiday House. Four parts, for strings. Ink has faded.

did.unittitle: Nacre for Flute and Piano (Original version 1925)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Original and revised copy of Nacre. Handwritten.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Revisions of score and parts for flute version of Nacre

did.unittitle: Oh, Had I Wings [motet for chorus]
did.physdesc: 2 black ink manuscripts with blue edits

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One incomplete manuscript and one complete with edited words and music. No dates.

did.unittitle: The Phoenix - Original Pencil Score/motives notes/revision notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: several versions of The Phoenix, in pencil

did.unittitle: O God Our Help in Ages Past

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: one copy

odd.head: General note
odd.p: "This special setting dedicated to Raymond Moremen and The A Capella Choir of UCLA"

did.unittitle: Sym. #2 (Vincent) Engraved score [from which 500 copies were printed Spring 1976]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Symphony no. 2 (Consort) for piano and string orchestra; includes score and piano, piano solo with orchestra reduction and string orchestra parts (in sets or separately)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: "Cameo Music Reproduction" score: Finale from Quintet for Piano & Strings. Additional copy in box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: "Cameo Music Reproduction" score: Yellowed score with revisions

did.physdesc: 6 individual scores (bound) with gold printed title on cover
8 individual scores (bound) with gold printed title on cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 7 photocopied reproductions of score in binding; with invoice from Belwin Mills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 5 Reproductions of score in binding; with invoice from Belwin Mills.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 9 bound copies of Stabat Mater. Some copies have pencil corrections.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 1 vellum master copy of score; 1 leatherbound edition with phoenix emblazoned on cover; 3 additional bound (not leather) editions

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Commissioned by the Phoenix Symphony Association; Guy Taylor, conductor.

did.unittitle: Primeval Void - 1 typed libretto (J.V. work copy) and 2 typed libretti

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Three copies of Primeval Void. One is stapled and has pencil marks on it. The other two are in plastic sheets.

did.unittitle: Documents relating to musical works, A-O
Three Jacks: Complete Ballet with Fanfare
did.physdesc: Original parts and copies (no master score)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Multiple copies of individual parts for the Three Jacks. Ink on paper.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Note in box: For Royce Hall Production LA Chamber Symphony Premiere; Instrumentation: Flute, oboe, clarinet, bassoon, horn, trumpet, trombone, percussion, violin, viola, cello, bass

did.unittitle: Primeval Void (Original Rough Sketch; Print of rough sketch)
did.physdesc: photocopy of sketch and pencil manuscript originals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One photocopy of piece, original manuscript sketches, plus handwritten (pencil) libretto, plus cast of characters.

did.unittitle: Primeval Void (Pencil Piano and Vocal Score; Reduction of Orch. Score)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil piano and vocal score (reduction of orchestra score); part (story, libretto and music) by JV and part by Chodchi Ahw

odd.head: General note
odd.p: No dates. Still has tentative title of Primeval Void, so probably early in composition's history.

did.physdesc: One copy of score of Primeval Void.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 56 page paste-up of score, ready for printing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Sketches of Quintet.

did.unittitle: Quintet (1960) Rough sketches for Mvt I, II, III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketches and revisions.

did.unittitle: Retrograde Canon for Alma Mahler-Werfel on her 70th Birthday-Aug. 31, 1949

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketch.

did.unittitle: Rondo Rhapsody (Original Pencil Sketch) Completed Easter Sunday '65 Premiere Washington May 9, 1965

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketches and copies of manuscript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Copy of piece: Shenando'h (for Treble Voices)

did.unittitle: Sing Holly-Loo [solo voice and piano] MS and xerox reductions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two versions. Piece also credits Byron Herbert Reece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketch.

did.unittitle: Soliloquy for cello solo and strings (score) -some changes in cello part since the masters were copied

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Ink manuscript with pencil edits: Soliloquy for Cello and Strings (from String Quartet)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Various handwritten songs, such as Home on the Range, All the Pretty Little Horses, Silver Bells

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Folder says in pencil: Original materials for following book: Sing Together the Songs We Love by Ruth and John Vincent. This book got as far as corrected green galley, but the publishing company AMT folded just before the printing. A Carl Fischer representative recently expressed an interest. Where are the green galleys? I'd like to prepare the book for presentation to publisher.

did.unittitle: String Quartet #2 Complete Original Sketch Finished Feb. 6, 1967. Premier Louisville 1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil manuscript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Original ink manuscript with some pencil editing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Original paper manuscript with some editing and paper flaps over sections.

did.unittitle: Three Jacks a Ballet (Original Pencil Score) 1942 or 1943

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Versions of ballet sections, including The House That Jack Built

did.unittitle: Three Jacks Ballet (Orch. Score with fanfare) Unbound 1941

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketches of full score. No individual parts.

did.unittitle: School Fuge on a Gedalge Subject (incomplete)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: A- in blue pencil on cover. Final, incomplete, version of the piece.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil sketches of individual parts.

did.unittitle: Two Pieces for Cello and Piano (Concert Piece; Cygnet)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Score and parts

did.unittitle: Victory Salute (pencil copy) for brass ensemble (4 trpt., 3 horns, 3 tromb., tuba)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil master (all parts); ink individual parts.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Piece says John Vincent (1902-1977) on top; copied after his death?

did.unittitle: Trifle from Suite for Orchestra 1929 (Original pencil sketch, reverse original)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Various parts of Trifle.

did.unittitle: Trifle: Piano reduction of wind parts 1929

did.unittitle: Trifle: 1 score duplicate of original 2 piano reduction Short score use for making band arrangements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of master and parts with red pencil markings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Ink/paper piano piece with no title or date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Incomplete passacaglia.

did.unittitle: Chacone (for Violin and Piano): August Durand, op.68. Freely transcribed from the original piano solo and arranged for violin and piano by John Vincent.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Hand written manuscript of the Chacone by Durand arranged by Vincent.

did.unittitle: Chaconne fo [sic] Piano Solo. John Vincent.
did.unitdate: none given: same physical appearance as 1930s manuscripts.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Inside says: Chaconne for Pianoforte Solo.

did.unittitle: Original MS of 3rd movement of Consort for Piano and Strings (piano quintet); plus manuscript of entire piece.
Concerto Grosso for strings and piano- score for 1st mov

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes different versions and arrangements including; Faculty Men's Glee Club, drafts, and a "personal copy" of Cindy for male chorus with baritone solo

did.unittitle: The Calf (for high voice and piano)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: only one version of the song, handwritten in ink on paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains Canon for String Trio with Free accompaniment; no individual parts. Handwritten in ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two photocopies of the piece, plus personal Christmas card from the Vincents that has the same piece in red print.

did.unittitle: Ballad of the Bones (Voice and Piano) [incomplete, I think]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Single page manuscript for voice and piano. Handwritten in ink on paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of various works by Handel and Vivaldi. No original work by Vincent.

did.unittitle: Behold the Star (from incidental music for "The Hallowed Time") Voice and Piano

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Empty folder. Music is missing?

did.unittitle: A Christmas Psalm - versions for male voices and for mixed chorus (1969)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of piece as standard four part hymn; two handwritten (pencil) manuscripts for male voices and mixed chorus, respectively.

did.unittitle: Consort for Piano and String Quartet (or Quintet, or String Orchestra) Masters of Parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum score.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Galleys from publisher of Consort with Vincent's pencil corrections and remarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Sealed envelope containing music for "The Hallowed Time," with notes indicating not to open ("this is intended as a form of copyright. The Registry is sealed and will establish the age of the work as of the registry date").

did.unittitle: Fragment from Horace: For Franklin Murphy [SATB A Cappella] Pencil sketches/xeroxed copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Various sketches of piece along with one photocopied copy of completed work.

did.unittitle: The Hallowed Time 1 score (A Cappella SATB chorus)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Incidental music for Richard Hubler's Christmas Play The Hallowed Time by John Vincent. SATB arrangement. Not original. Says, in ink, "Property of John Vincent" on the cover.

did.unittitle: Glory to God (Male Chorus) Pencil Sketches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two copies of piece: one in ink with pencil edits (for solo male quartet and male chorus) and one entirely in pencil (male chorus version)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Ink version inscription: For Paul Hume and his Chorus/Christmas 1957

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil drafts of various movements of the piece, including stage directions.

did.unittitle: How Shall We Sing? (for SAB chorus and piano) 1944

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Multiple copies of complete score.

did.unittitle: How Shall We Sing the Lord's Song Pencil sketches/original MS [Mixed Voices a Cappella] 1951

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum and paper versions of work in pencil and ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: original manuscript. Note on cover: 1st part later completely rewritten, 2nd part, almost intact, 3rd part, revised.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: 3 bound copies of manuscript with pencil edits

did.unittitle: Who Will Kiss Your Ruby Lips (1 complete score and xerox of various pages) [SATB + piano] LA 8-29-50 Revised 2-13-51

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Edited masters and sketches.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Cover says: Who Will Kiss Your Ruby Lips for a Cappella Chorus American folksong in a setting by John Vincent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Pencil and ink on vellum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Individual parts for Primeval Void.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: In envelope mailed from the Portland Junior Symphony. Postmarked 3/4/76

did.unittitle: Suite of Benjamin Franklin [transcription for string orchestra with obligato for glass armonica]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One copy.

did.physdesc: Fragile cotton sheets; housed in hard folder with striped belt/clasp

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Baritone solo, chorus & orchestra Based on a folk song

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Dated with "Bowling Green" location?

did.unittitle: Lord Arling Master of Orch. Parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Ink on vellum. No master score, just individual parts.

did.unittitle: Madrigal-master and Ma Dame aux Doux Yeux-master

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: One master of each piece.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: No dates.

did.unittitle: Music for Red Cross Film (1948) - masters of string parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters of individual parts.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Parts: Violin I, Violin II, Violas, Celli, Bassi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Masters for each instrument. No score.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Masters for each brass and percussion parts. No master score.

did.unittitle: Nacre - flute and piano - masters of 1925 version

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters of Nacre, reduction for flute and piano.

did.unittitle: Lord Arling Overture Score Masters Dec 6, 1959

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters of Lord Arling Overture. No individual parts, just master score.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Piece is either entitled Horace:Odes or For Franklin Murphy. It's not clear. 4 part vocal (SATB). One page total.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Master for Cygnet, for cello and piano. Two pages. Ink with pencil notes.

did.unittitle: Cindy for baritone solo, TTBB chorus, and piano (master)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Master and parts for Cindy-Gal. Scored for baritone, TTBB and piano.

did.unittitle: Cindy for baritone solo, TTBB chorus, and piano (reverse master)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Score for Cindy-Gal. Reverse master--black paper with white text.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Transparent masters of individual parts for Consort of Strings with Piano

did.unittitle: Symphonic Poem After Descartes - 2 piano version masters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two versions of piece, all in pencil on vellum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Complete master of Symphony on vellum in ink and pencil. Also started paper copy of piano reduction.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum master of piano reduction of Symphony in D. Incomplete.

did.unittitle: Symphony in D - masters of orchestral parts for temporary revision of Feb. 1955

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Transparent masters; edits made with paper glued atop the vellum. Includes alternate endings.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Note from JV: Sym in D: masters of orchestra parts (changes only) of Feb. 55 revision Sym in D: Masters for changes in parts from earliest version to the revision dated Feb. 1955 (This is not the final revision) These partial masters were for the purpose of incorporating the temporary revisions into the earliest versions of the orchestra parts

did.unittitle: Three Jacks A Ballet (arr. for piano solo) MASTER

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Two transparent masters: 1 is the full ballet for piano solo, 2 is "Jack Spratt," part of the ballet for strings and piano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum master of treble part.

did.unittitle: Soliloquy for Cello Solo and Strings (Masters for score and parts)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters with some minor edits.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Transcription of the Second Movement of String Quartet No. 1.

did.unittitle: Suite for Percussion (and piano)/ Masters of score and parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Written for 6 parts. Duration is 3 minutes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum master of score. No individual parts.

did.unittitle: Quintet (1951) Masters Superseded version of first movement Score & Parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Score and individual parts.

did.unittitle: Recitative (Soliloquy) for cello and strings - copying never completed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum master of score for string quartet. It is not finished.

did.unittitle: String Quartet #2 Holograph transparency score and parts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters of score and parts with copies of the same. Edits made on copies.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Slip of paper that says: String Quartet #2 Title Page and pp1-4 on display 4/2/77 Ron Weschler

did.physdesc: Vellum masters and masters for print (shiny paper)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Vellum masters of score and parts with shiny masters of same.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Postcard announcing performance by the American Art Quartet

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Handwritten noted that Victor Gottlieb, cellist, was Shapiro's late husband. No dates.
